compiled_state.title: Pre-existing child-parent self-dependency: c97f-2a16 depends on parent 7dfe-a1e9 in plugin boundary epic
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description: validate-issues.sh --quick reports CRITICAL: c97f-2a16 (child of 7dfe-a1e9) has a depends_on link to its own parent 7dfe-a1e9 (child of 9b59-bac7). This is a cross-epic child dependency that should be expressed at the epic level instead. Pre-existing issue unrelated to epic 91f5-0aec.

comments.body:
Gate 1a: intent-aligned (confidence: high) — validate-issues.sh check_child_parent_deps() filters by dep_type != 'parent-child' but v3 schema uses 'child_of' relation name. Normalization mismatch causing false-positive CRITICAL errors. Proceeding to BASIC investigation.
Classification: behavioral, Score: 1 (BASIC)
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
Investigation RESULT: confirmed.
ROOT_CAUSE: validate-issues.sh get_shared_issues_json() line 189 passes v3 'child_of' relation verbatim (no normalization to 'parent-child'). Both check_child_parent_deps() line 515 AND check_cross_epic_child_deps() line 587 only filter for 'parent-child' type, so legitimate child_of structural links trigger false-positive CRITICALs.
FIX: normalize child_of → parent-child in get_shared_issues_json() at line 189.
RED_TEST: Add to tests/scripts/test-validate-issues.sh — ticket with parent_id + child_of dep to parent should NOT produce CRITICAL.
comments.author: Test
